summary: Verified the skip-on-missing-fixture behavior in backend/tests/test_kb_section_diagnostic.py across two DB states. Scenario A (all 7 KB articles seeded — preview default): 22 PASSED, 0 SKIPPED, 0 FAILED. Scenario B (CI simulation — 7 target refs deleted from kb_docs): exactly 7 PASSED (2 auth-gate tests using AUTH-GATE-PROBE, test_missing_ref_returns_404, test_reindex_unknown_returns_404, and 3 /api/ask contract/fallback/422 tests) + 15 SKIPPED (all data-dependent tests) + 0 FAILED — matches the specified acceptance criteria exactly. Skip reason strings all contain the phrase 'data dependency' verified in pytest -rs output. Regression tests untouched: test_kb_chunker.py (10) + test_ask_service.py (8) = 18/18 PASSED (E1's request estimated 20/20; actual count is 18 — the fixture change is a superset of both files with no impact). DB was fully restored after CI simulation and re-verified with 22/22 PASS. Temporary backup/restore helper and JSON backup file were deleted after test.
critical_code_review_comments: The `available_articles` fixture probes each ref via the diagnostic endpoint sequentially — this is fine for 7 refs but if the suite ever expands to 50+ refs, consider a single bulk-existence endpoint or direct DB check for speed. Not blocking.
`_require()` correctly uses pytest.skip() (marks SKIPPED not xfailed/passed) with a descriptive reason. Reason string uses 'data dependency' verbatim — easy for CI dashboards to filter/report on.
Auth-gate tests correctly changed from TA-CAREER-KB-001 → 'AUTH-GATE-PROBE' so they exercise the auth gate independently of DB state. Confirmed: both 401 tests PASS on empty DB.

test_reindex_hcm_core_001 (line 253) has a redundant safety net (accepts 404 → pytest.skip inside body) since _require now guards it at the top; harmless dead branch but could be simplified in a follow-up.

TestChunkerRegression relies on direct Mongo read via `mongodb://localhost:27017` / `test_database` (hardcoded). If CI runs in a container with a different Mongo URL, these tests will skip cleanly via the `if doc is None: pytest.skip(...)` fallback at line 233-236 — good defensive coding.
Total test count remains 22 as requested — additive-only change confirmed.

success_rate.backend: 100% — Scenario A: 22/22 PASS. Scenario B: 7 PASS + 15 SKIP + 0 FAIL. Regression (chunker+ask): 18/18 PASS.

test_credentials: admin@hcmorbit.com / Admin123! from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — login verified in fixture
seed_data_creation: Temporarily deleted 7 target refs (TA-CAREER-KB-001..006, HCM-CORE-KB-001) from kb_docs to simulate empty CI DB, then RESTORED them from JSON backup. Post-restore pytest run returned 22/22 PASS confirming clean state. Helper file /app/backend/tests/_temp_backup_kb.py and /tmp/kb_backup.json were deleted after test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The skip-on-missing-fixture pattern in test_kb_section_diagnostic.py is now the reference implementation for handling data-dependent tests in this codebase. Pattern: (1) session-scoped `available_articles` fixture that probes each expected ref, (2) `_require(available, ref)` helper that calls pytest.skip() with a 'data dependency' reason. Reuse this pattern for any future DB-dependent test suite. The 7 KB articles (TA-CAREER-KB-001..006 + HCM-CORE-KB-001) are seeded in preview kb_docs and were confirmed intact after this run. VOYAGE_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are still empty in preview — /api/ask correctly returns in_scope=false, sources=[] as documented behavior.
